nameMeanings.name: Nayte
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Neat
meanings: English: from Middle English neat ‘ox, cow’ (Old English nēat), probably applied as a metonymic occupational name for a cowherd, or a nickname for someone thought to bear some resemblance to the animal. The modern English adjective neat (via French from Latin nitidus ‘clean, shining’) does not occur before the 16th century, after the main period of surname formation.

nameMeanings.name: Miss
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname derived from the usual medieval vernacular form of   Moses  , or a revival of this form. In Wales it has in recent years also been used as a short form of Mostyn .

en.lifeSummary: Miss Nayte was born in 1405, in Wakefield St Mary, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. She married Thomas Lister in 1424, in Wakefield St Mary, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons.
